brand_identity.name: LITS (Love In The Sky)
brand_identity.target_audience: Pilots, Flight Attendants, Ramp Agents, Operations Agents, Provision Agents, Aviation Mechanics, Ground Service Equipment Operators

headings.usage: Use for all major section headers, hero text, and modal titles. Tracking should be tight (-0.02em) for large text.

body.usage: Use for all interface text, chat bubbles, and schedule details. Highly legible at small sizes.

technical.usage: Use sparingly for flight numbers (e.g., UA245), gate info, and time zones to reinforce the airline aesthetic.

usage_rules: Use the 'Twilight Gradient' (Cyan to Violet) for primary actions and active states.
Backgrounds must be deep slate/navy (#020617), never pure black, to reduce eye strain in low-light cockpits/cabins.
Use 'Gold' accents strictly for 'Captain/Premium' features or high-value matches.
Text fields and cards must use high-opacity glassmorphism (bg-slate-900/80) to maintain readability over vibrant backgrounds.

layout_strategy.grid_system: Mobile-first 4-column grid, expanding to 12-column bento grid on desktop.

containers.modal_style: Bottom-sheet on mobile, centered dialog on desktop. Heavy shadow (shadow-2xl).

core_components.name: BoardingPassCard
core_components.description: A card styled like a digital boarding pass representing a match or a schedule item.

core_components.name: RadarPulse
core_components.description: A circular radar sweep animation for the 'Discovery' or 'Nearby' page.
core_components.style: Green/Cyan scanning line on a dark circular grid.

core_components.name: ScheduleTimeline
core_components.description: Horizontal scrolling timeline showing layover windows.

transitions.page_transition: Slide up (like a takeoff) or Fade in

images_and_assets.guidelines: Use high-contrast, moody images. Night scenes preferred. Cockpit lights, runway bokeh, city skylines at dusk.

selected_assets.description: Pilot in cockpit looking at runway - Use for Login/Hero background
Cocktail on rooftop at night - Use for 'Layover Discovery' cards
Runway lights at dusk - Use for footer or subtle texture overlays

instructions_to_main_agent.general: You are building a high-energy, dark-mode mobile app. Prioritize visual impact and 'cool factor'. Avoid corporate starkness.
setup_steps: Configure Tailwind colors to match the 'Twilight Palette'.
Set up the font imports for Outfit and Be Vietnam Pro.
Create the 'BoardingPassCard' component first as it sets the design language.
specific_rules: Do NOT use pure black (#000000). Use #020617.
Inputs must look like cockpit instruments (dark bg, subtle glow on focus).
Use a 'Radar' motif for loading states instead of a spinner.

UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
Use 'sonner' for toasts. Sonner component are located in /app/frontend/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx
